channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump administration flies 10-year-old back from Cuba amid custody fight
item.description: President Trump's Department of Justice sent a plane this week to Cuba to return a 10-year-old from Utah who is at the center of a custody fight involving the child's gender identity.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump's Department of Justice sent a plane this week to Cuba to return a 10-year-old from Utah who is at the center of a custody fight involving the child's gender identity.

(Image credit: Hannah Schoenbaum)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Chemical leak at a W.Va. plant kills 2 people, sends 30 more to hospitals, officials say
item.description: The leak occurred at the Catalyst Refiners plant, a silver recovery business. An emergency management official says workers were preparing to shut down at least part of the facility when the leak occurred, causing a chemical gas reaction.
item.content:encoded:
The leak occurred at the Catalyst Refiners plant, a silver recovery business. An emergency management official says workers were preparing to shut down at least part of the facility when the leak occurred, causing a chemical gas reaction.

(Image credit: John Raby)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Pentagon says Navy secretary is leaving, the latest departure of a top defense leader
item.description: Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell said John Phelan, the Navy's top civilian official, was "departing the administration, effective immediately." Navy Undersecretary Hung Cao will become acting secretary of the Navy.
item.content:encoded:
Pentagon spokesman Sean Parnell said John Phelan, the Navy's top civilian official, was "departing the administration, effective immediately." Navy Undersecretary Hung Cao will become acting secretary of the Navy.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Tesla's making money. But it's planning to spend an awful lot more
item.description: Tesla's profits were up from this time last year. But the company warned investors to prepare for expensive investments in next-generation technology like humanoid robots and AI.
item.content:encoded:
Tesla's profits were up from this time last year. But the company warned investors to prepare for expensive investments in next-generation technology like humanoid robots and AI.

(Image credit: Jenny Kane)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: The tariff refund process has begun for businesses. What about customers?
item.description: While shipping companies are pledging refunds for customers who directly paid tariff fees, the situation is much trickier for retailers.
item.content:encoded:
While shipping companies are pledging refunds for customers who directly paid tariff fees, the situation is much trickier for retailers.

(Image credit: Jenny Kane)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Inside Linda McMahon's effort to dismantle the Department of Education
item.description: A former pro-wrestling executive, McMahon is now the education secretary Trump tasked with abolishing the agency. New Yorker writer Zach Helfand explains how her WWE experience led her to this role.
item.content:encoded: A former pro-wrestling executive, McMahon is now the education secretary Trump tasked with abolishing the agency. New Yorker writer Zach Helfand explains how her WWE experience led her to this role.
item.dc:creator: Dave Davies

item.title: Iranians are leaving the country just to access the internet
item.description: Iran has cut off its access to the global internet. To find an internet connection, some Iranians are traveling across the border with Turkey — even just to make video calls and then go back home.
item.content:encoded:
Iran has cut off its access to the global internet. To find an internet connection, some Iranians are traveling across the border with Turkey — even just to make video calls and then go back home.

(Image credit: Pavel Nemecek)
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Gene therapy for a rare type of deafness shows lasting results
item.description: Researchers say a gene therapy allowed deaf children and adults as old as 32 to hear for the first time. The benefits have persisted for more than two years for some patients.
item.content:encoded: Researchers say a gene therapy allowed deaf children and adults as old as 32 to hear for the first time. The benefits have persisted for more than two years for some patients.
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein

item.title: Family influencers make the lifestyle look good. But kids pay the price, new book says
item.description: What does it mean to monetize your offspring? To turn their childhood into content? In Like, Follow, Subscribe Fortesa Latifi explores what drives parents to become family influencers.
item.content:encoded: What does it mean to monetize your offspring? To turn their childhood into content? In Like, Follow, Subscribe Fortesa Latifi explores what drives parents to become family influencers.
item.dc:creator: Ilana Masad

item.title: Greetings from an Islamabad park, a peaceful vantage point in an uncertain world
item.description: The park, near the venue where inconclusive Iran-U.S. peace talks took place this month, provides respite to those who visit it.
item.content:encoded: The park, near the venue where inconclusive Iran-U.S. peace talks took place this month, provides respite to those who visit it.
item.dc:creator: Betsy Joles
